texto: It was too much happiness. Happiness puts you at too much risk - what if you were to lose it? Too much happiness is a paradox. It's a tragedy, even: getting something you've always wanted but being unable to keep it.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jowita Bydlowska que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “It was too much happiness. Happiness puts you at too much risk - what if you were to lose it? Too…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, memoir, happiness, com foco em reflexao.

texto: There's this parallel, perhaps less conscious desire, which is to numb myself to the world. To deal with the world tomorrow. Living is difficult. Dying is difficult.
